title: release-notes prose
description: The ONLY output of the polish agent. Prose strings that fill the deterministic page template. The agent writes no headings, tables, banners, @handles, hearts or PR links — those are rendered from data.json.

theme.description: 2-6 words naming what THIS release is about, e.g. 'First stable v4 release'. Rendered bold in the banner. No trailing punctuation.

highlights_headline.description: ONE sentence, <=20 WORDS (the renderer enforces the cap in words, not characters): the single most important thing about this release. Not a list.

highlights_body.description: Optional. <=60 WORDS (renderer enforces in words) naming 2-3 themes in prose. No PR links, no @handles, no comma-run enumeration of every change. Null when the headline says enough.

breaking.description: One entry per behavioural or signature breaking change a consumer must act on. Empty array renders 'None in this release.' Titles/bodies only — PR links are added from prs[].

categories.description: Curated product-facing sections. Only headings from data.allowed_categories. Each bullet MERGES related PRs into one theme; the agent supplies lead+detail prose and the PR numbers, the template renders links and community ❤️ credit. Aim 3-5 bullets per section — curate, don't enumerate.

contributor_summaries.description: One entry per login in data.contributors (roster). Value = one line summarising that person's contributions in prose, e.g. 'Variable fonts, singleton lifecycle rework and the SKPath finalizer fix'. The template adds the @handle and PR links.

preview_summaries.description: One entry per preview/RC key in data.previews. Value = 1-2 sentences summarising what that milestone shipped. The template adds the heading, date and changelog link.
